dialogos.texto_en: Oh my, it's Fu-kun.
Nino and Ichika, eh.\nSeems you haven't tried to run off to the cafe yet.
We haven't.\nThere are more interesting things here than I thought.
Nino had been glued\nto the traditional clothing exhibit over there\nfor a while.
As long as you guys are enjoying yourself,\nthat's all that matters.
Speaking of which, I was thinking about it when we entered,\nbut doesn't this building have a strange shape?
It's all white and it's tiered.\nIt has a pyramid-like feel to it,\nbut it's definitely not a pyramid, right?
Yeah, I don't think so.
The brochure says it's modeled after a gusuku,\nbut what exactly is a gusuku?
Gusuku is the word for 'castle' in Okinawa. Well,\neven though we call it a castle, it's a bit different\nfrom what we think of as castles in mainland Japan.
Oh, a castle.\nNow that you mention it,\nit does have some similarities with the Shurijo Castle.
That place is also tiered inside.\nI remember I had to frequently climb up stairs.
Yeah, that's why there's tiers.
I get it now, so a gusuku is a castle.\nAhh%p-2;――%p;.
...What's up?\nWhy are you looking at me?
Miku might be interested in that kind of thing.\nShe'd be happy if you told her.
Ooh...
So, about Miku.\nI was meaning to ask you earlier, Ichika.
So, hey?\nAbout that%p-2;――
So you have any idea why Miku is acting strangely?
You guys asked me earlier,\nbut I was curious\nif you guys had any clues, or anything.
I said it earlier too, but if there's a reason,\nit's probably you, Fu-kun, more than us.
Yeah, I think so too.
Really?
Well, for example,\nmaybe Miku didn't want to go to Okinawa.\nThat's a possible reason, right?
That's out.
Obviously.
I don't think so.
Yeah, me neither.\nThat's why I asked you.
Really?
Well, for example,\nmaybe Miku didn't want to go to Okinawa.\nThat's a possible reason, right?
That's out.
Obviously.
An immediate answer, huh.
Well, when you mentioned Okinawa,\nMiku also agreed, right?
Maybe she was holding back.\nCause you guys were so excited.
We don't have the relationship\nwhere she'd hold back like that.
Besides, if she were really holding back,\nwe wouldn't have been able to decide\non a travel destination so easily.
But you know,\nit seems like Miku's enjoying the trip herself.
Yeah.\nShe's the same as usual when she's talking to us.
Well, that's a relief...
Maybe the habu snakes scared her off.
What's wrong, you two just went quiet.
Wait, do you guys really have a fear of habu snakes?\nOr just snakes in general?
PFT! AHAHAHAH!\nFutaro-kun, you are just TOO off the mark!
Why are you laughing?!
Don't worry, I've never heard her say anything about\nbeing afraid of snakes, and she's never said a word\nabout being afraid of habu snakes either.
Ahh%p-2;――%p; goodness.\nSometimes you say the most outrageous things,\nit's amusing.
(That wasn't my intention...)
But you know,\nit seems like Miku's enjoying the trip herself.
Yeah.\nShe's the same as usual when she's talking to us.
Well, that's a relief...
In that case, why...?
Isn't it your job to think about it,\nFutaro-kun?
...You're not saying anything at all.
Of course, I think you're doing your best.
It's nice of you to say that.
You're surprisingly straightforward.
...Seeing Miku lately makes me anxious.\nBut, I feel like she's either feeling more anxious than me or worried about something else closely related.
Knowing that I-I...\nAs her boyfriend, I can't do anything about it.
If you truly care that much about her, Fu-kun,\nthere must be a way to solve this issue.
That's right.\nPull yourself together, boyfriend.
But you know,\nit seems like Miku's enjoying the trip herself.
Yeah.\nShe's the same as usual when she's talking to us.
Well, that's a relief...
But,\nthat's only when she's with you, right?
...So that would mean,\nthere's a high possibility it's my fault.\nBut I really can't think of any reasons, you know?
What may be nothing to you, Fu-kun,\ncould be something that makes Miku feel\nlike the world is ending...
W-What should I do?!
Teehee!\nDid you finally figure it out, Fu-kun?
What?
That 'love' is very complex.
...Yeah.\nI think I got it.
Well, probably, but, compared to you guys I'm still nowhere\nclose to understanding what Miku's feeling.
Hang in there, Futaro-kun.
